mnemonics.title: CMM Levels Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: I PR主动 (I=P Initial, P=Repeatable, R=Defined, Zh=Quantitatively Managed, Dong=Optimizing) - Imagine 'I Please Read Zhao's Notes' in Chinese pattern. Initial→Repeatable→Defined→Quantitatively Managed→Optimizing. Use: 'I Prefer Reading Quality Orbits' - Initial, Repeatable, Defined, Quantitatively, Optimizing

mnemonics.title: DMAIC Sequence
mnemonics.content: Don't Make Arguments In Court (Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control) - Remember the Six Sigma DMAIC cycle order.

mnemonics.title: TQM Principles
mnemonics.content: CFID: Customer focus, Foresight/Forward thinking, Involvement of people, Data/Defect-free goals - Use: 'Customers Find Improved Defects' or 'Customer First, Everyone Involved, Daily Improvement'

acronyms.usage: Framework for process improvement combining multiple CMM models

acronyms.expansion: Software Process Improvement and Capability dEtermination
acronyms.usage: ISO/IEC 15504 standard for software process assessment
Management philosophy for organizational quality improvement
Iterative cycle for continuous improvement (Deming cycle)
Six Sigma methodology for process improvement
Set of related processes achieving important goals in CMM/CMMI
Systematic evaluation of software development processes

visualTips.visualization: Visualize a 5-level pyramid: Base (Level 1) is wide and unstable, Level 2-4 form the middle section, and Level 5 is the narrow peak representing optimization. Each level builds upon the previous.

Think of Continuous representation as a 'pick and choose' buffet where you select individual processes to improve. Staged representation is like a 'fixed course meal' where you must complete all processes at each level before moving up.
Imagine a circular flow chart: Plan (top), Do (right), Check (bottom), Act (left). Arrows show continuous rotation indicating ongoing improvement.

Maturity is like your age (overall development), Capability is like your current performance in a specific subject. You can be mature but have low capability in some areas, or highly capable in one area despite overall lower maturity.

analogies.analogy: Like human development: Level 1 (Initial) = Infant - unpredictable; Level 2 (Managed) = Child - follows routines; Level 3 (Defined) = Teenager - follows rules; Level 4 (Quantitatively Managed) = Adult - makes data-driven decisions; Level 5 (Optimizing) = Wise Elder - continuously improves self and others.

Like maintaining a car: Regular assessments (service checks), fixing problems (repairs), preventive measures (oil changes), and upgrades (new parts) - all part of continuous improvement to keep running smoothly.
Like sports training - you study what top athletes do (best practices), compare your performance to theirs, and adopt their techniques to improve your own performance.
Like calculating accident rate per 1000 drivers - gives a normalized quality measure that accounts for different sizes of projects.
Like a teacher's assessment rubric - it has specific criteria (process attributes) and rating scales to objectively evaluate how well processes are performed.
